ontology.foodon_gap_note: No FoodOn term exists specifically for Ali Nazik Lamb. FOODON_00002764 (lamb dish) is used as the closest parent category.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: No FoodEx2 code exists for Ali Nazik as a composite dish. A00GJ (lamb meat, cooked) is used as the closest parent anchor for the dominant protein component.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT code found for Ali Nazik Lamb or a sufficiently close composite Turkish lamb dish.

serving.natural_language_portion: A standard restaurant serving of Ali Nazik Lamb is approximately 350 g, comprising a bed of smoked eggplant and garlic yogurt topped with sautéed or grilled lamb pieces and finished with spiced butter.

nutrient_source.title: USDA FoodData Central — Lamb, composite of trimmed retail cuts, cooked (NDB 17003) and Eggplant, cooked, boiled, drained, without salt (NDB 11210) and Yogurt, plain, whole milk (NDB 01116); composite estimation for Ali Nazik Lamb
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service, FoodData Central
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No single approved-source entry exists for Ali Nazik Lamb as a composite dish. Nutrient values are estimated from USDA FoodData Central component data: cooked lamb (dominant protein source, ~50% by weight), smoked/cooked eggplant (~30% by weight), and garlic yogurt (~15% by weight), with butter and spices (~5%). Weighted average applied per 100 g of finished dish. Energy cross-checked against NCC Food and Nutrient Database generic lamb-eggplant composite category.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2021 — Atkinson FS et al., American Journal of Clinical Nutrition; eggplant and yogurt components referenced; lamb contributes negligible GI
gi_source.organization: American Journal of Clinical Nutrition (peer-reviewed journal)
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for Ali Nazik Lamb as a composite dish. GI estimated at 35 (low) based on dominant low-GI components: cooked eggplant (GI ~15, Atkinson et al. 2021), whole-milk yogurt (GI ~36, Atkinson et al. 2021), and lamb meat (GI effectively 0 as a protein-fat food). Weighted composite GI calculated proportionally by carbohydrate contribution per component. Glycemic load per serving = (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (35 × 21.7) / 100 ≈ 7.6 (serving size 350 g, carbohydrate 6.2 g/100 g × 3.5 = 21.7 g).

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium content (~310 mg/100 g; ~1085 mg per serving)
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should be aware that a standard restaurant serving of Ali Nazik Lamb provides approximately 1085 mg sodium, representing roughly 47% of the 2300 mg daily limit recommended by major cardiovascular guidelines; portion control and requesting reduced-salt preparation are advisable.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Low glycemic index (~35) and moderate carbohydrate content (~6.2 g/100 g)
type2_diabetes.guidance: Ali Nazik Lamb has a low estimated glycemic index of approximately 35 and a glycemic load of approximately 7.6 per standard serving, making it a suitable choice for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes when consumed as part of a balanced meal; the yogurt and eggplant base contribute beneficial protein and fibre that moderate postprandial glucose response.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Saturated fat from lamb and butter (~3.4 g/100 g) and sodium load
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Ali Nazik Lamb contains moderate saturated fat (~11.9 g per serving) and high sodium (~1085 mg per serving), both of which are relevant cardiovascular risk factors; individuals with cardiovascular disease should limit portion size, request preparation with less butter, and balance the meal with low-sodium accompaniments.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Ali Nazik Lamb is a classic Ottoman and Turkish regional dish originating from Gaziantep in southeastern Turkey. The dish consists of tender grilled or sautéed lamb — typically minced or cubed — served atop a creamy bed of fire-roasted smoked eggplant (aubergine) blended with garlic-infused whole-milk yogurt. The preparation is finished with a drizzle of spiced clarified butter or olive oil, often seasoned with red pepper flakes (pul biber) and black pepper. The name 'Ali Nazik' translates loosely as 'gentle Ali' or 'delicate Ali' in Turkish, and the dish is celebrated for its contrasting textures: the smoky, silky eggplant-yogurt base against the savoury, slightly charred lamb. Nutritionally, Ali Nazik Lamb is a protein-rich dish (~11.8 g protein per 100 g) with moderate fat content (~8.5 g/100 g), predominantly from lamb and butter. The eggplant contributes dietary fibre (~1.4 g/100 g) and antioxidants, while the yogurt provides calcium (~68 mg/100 g) and beneficial probiotics. The dish has a low estimated glycemic index (~35) due to the dominance of protein, fat, and low-GI vegetables, making it relatively suitable for glycaemic management. Sodium content is elevated at approximately 310 mg/100 g in restaurant preparations, which is a consideration for individuals managing hypertension or cardiovascular disease. Ali Nazik is commonly served as a main course in Turkish restaurants worldwide, including in Singapore where it appears on menus at Turkish and Lebanese cuisine establishments. It is naturally gluten-free and halal-compliant.
common_user_queries: What is Ali Nazik made of?

confidence_reason: No single approved-source entry exists for Ali Nazik Lamb as a composite dish. Nutrient values are estimated from USDA FoodData Central component data for cooked lamb, cooked eggplant, and whole-milk yogurt using weighted proportional composition. GI is an analogous estimate based on peer-reviewed GI tables for the dominant low-GI components. Confidence is medium rather than low because the component sources are high-quality approved sources and the estimation methodology is transparent and defensible.
notes: 1. Nutrient values are estimated from USDA FoodData Central component data (cooked lamb ~50%, cooked eggplant ~30%, garlic yogurt ~15%, butter and spices ~5% by weight) as no single approved-source entry exists for Ali Nazik Lamb as a composite dish. 2. Vitamin D is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA FoodData Central entries for the component ingredients at the level of precision required for this composite estimation. 3. monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g, potassium_mg, calcium_mg, iron_mg, cholesterol_mg, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, and water_g are estimated from USDA FoodData Central component data and are reported as best-estimate values. 4. GI is an analogous estimate (GI ~35, low) based on Atkinson et al. 2021 (AJCN) GI values for cooked eggplant (~15) and whole-milk yogurt (~36); lamb contributes negligible GI as a protein-fat food. 5. Serving size of 350 g is estimated based on typical Turkish restaurant main-course portion norms; actual restaurant portions may vary. 6. The cuisine_tags 'Kebab' and 'Shawarma' from the input record are noted; Ali Nazik is more accurately classified as a kebab-adjacent dish (the lamb component is grilled/sautéed kebab-style) rather than a shawarma; the shawarma tag is not carried forward as it is not accurate for this dish. 7. This dish is served at Sultan Palace Turkish and Lebanese Cuisine in Singapore (Boat Quay) per field survey data.
